text:
This article is about the character from the Rebuild of Evangelion film series. For the character from Neon Genesis Evangelion, see Lilith.

 is the 2nd Angel belonging to the Rebuild of Evangelion film series. It was introduced in the Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone film. Unlike the other Angels in the Rebuild of Evangelion, the second Angel is named just like the Neon Genesis Evangelion counterpart.

Profile

Lilith appears to be a vaguely-formed roughly Evangelion-sized humanoid upper torso with blubbery white flesh, of non-distinct gender. Lilith has no legs or hips when it is seen in the year 2015, its torso terminating in masses with dozens of humanoid-sized pairs of legs growing out of them. Lilith is kept restrained in Terminal Dogma, crucified onto a giant red cross: large nails are driven through its palms into the cross from which it hangs, resembling the crucified Jesus. Lilith has five fingers on each hand (just like a human). Lilith remains totally immobile and nonreactive, simply hanging limp on the cross it's nailed to and unresponsive to events around it. Lilith's lower torso seemed to have the odd blobs with human-sized legs while impaled as rivaled to the blubber that grew into its torso after the Spear was removed and she could grow again.

Lilith's design in Rebuild had undergone an overhaul as well. No longer does Lilith have its trademark seven-eyed mask, but instead has an ossified mask or face-plate similar to the face Fourth Angel grows after the N 2 mine attack, with two nails with SEELE emblem in the eyeholes and a small beak protruding out of the lower end of the mask.

The Spear of Longinus is already impaled through its chest and the large bolts driven through its palms and into the cross remain. However, Lilith's chest bears a large scar across it, one that resembles the scar seen on Misato's chest in the TV series in both location and appearance. In addition, a series of small cross-shaped objects are driven into its chest. Lilith's cluster of legs now twitch and sway, and it continues to trickle LCL into the lake below.

History

Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone

Lilith appears again in Rebuild of Evangelion, although the time and circumstances of its appearance onscreen are vastly different from the TV series. Lilith is seen roughly three-fourths of the way through Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone. Instead of Kaji revealing Lilith to Misato, Misato herself shows Lilith to Shinji in order to explain the urgency of fighting the Sixth Angel, and correctly identifies Lilith as such. Dialogue between Gendo and Fuyutsuki reveals that a "contract" has been established between SEELE and Lilith that involves the destruction of all the Angels, for purposes involving the Human Instrumentality Project. When Kaworu is observing the Earth from the Moon, a being similar to the original Lilith is laying in a square hole in front of him, which is later revealed to be Mark.06 under construction in Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance.

Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance

Lilith is seen during the abortive Third Impact at the end of Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance, with what appears to be a faint glow and small white sparks floating off its body. Keel Lorenz, in an earlier conversation with Gendo, speaks about "resurrecting" Lilith. It is possible that Lilith was in some way affected by the events surrounding Unit-01 at the end of Evangelion: 2.0 You Can (Not) Advance, though to what extent is at present unknown.

Evangelion 3.0: You Can (Not) Redo

In Evangelion 3.0: You Can (Not) Redo Lilith's body is now much gigantic in size, and is in the midst of a pit filled with giant skulls, decomposing and in crawling position. It is pierced by two Spear of Longinus and beheaded (its head being replaced by Evangelion Mark.06). Its head, for its part, was in the former NERV Command Center, where it transformed into the face of Rei Ayanami. When Shinji withdrew the two spears from Evangelion 13 to prevent the Fourth Impact, its whole body reverted to LCL, including the head present in the Command Center.

Evangelion: 3.0+1.0 Thrice Upon A Time

In Evangelion: 3.0+1.0 Thrice Upon A Time, a flashback is shown during the conversation between Misato and Ritsuko, it shows the Evangelion Mark.06 holding Lilith's decapitated head and its body, delivered from the cross, still standing, and surrounded by gigantic skulls falling into the chamber where she was kept. Later during their fight in the Minus Space, Gendo shows to Shinji the Evangelion Imaginary which is perceived by Shinji as a black Lilith, its overall appearance being the same as it was in the first two films but colored black instead of white.

Development

The redesign of Lilith was initially made by okama, who reimagined it, dated from 21 March 2007 it depicts the Angel still using a mask that was similar to the old one but incorporating new ideas. The mask can be seen partially falling off, showing multiple eyes being hold by their optic nerve. The body would have an umbilical chord hanging it down, numerous square shaped cracks and cuts all over the body. The body would release a substance that would cause the creation of stalagmites around the cross. The arms are longer in this version, being held by nails under the end of the arms and the armpits. The hands are not pierced directly in palm and little arms can be seen sprouting just like the lower body with legs. Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone - Complete Records Visual Story Edition - Setting Material Edition p.309 

Another proposal was designed by Ikuto Yamashita, who imagined it by making an open incision in the skin of the Second Angel Lilith's whole torso being held by six nails directly at the cross. A cable can be seen dangling from the umbilical chord position and at the center of the chest a hole can be seen. The mask has two nails in the position of the eyes with the SEELE emblem in the middle of the mask. Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone - Complete Records Visual Story Edition - Setting Material Edition p.310 

Hideaki Anno corrected the designs for the final designs, at first he used the mask with the impact marks and the SEELE Emblem at the center and the beak-like protusion near the mouth position. Later he corrected it again and removed the impact marks and changed the SEELE emblem from the center to each nail in the eyes. He also closed the chest scar and made it similar to a surgery where it was closed already, leaving simple scars. The design ended simpler than the ones presented in the concept arts, being quite similar to the original Lilith design from the TV series. Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone - Complete Records Visual Story Edition - Setting Material Edition p.310 

However, elements from the earlier designs like the square shaped cracks, filllaments sprouting from the body, the presence of bones are present in Evangelion: 3.0 You Can (Not) Redo.
